stories.title: Google Vids now lets you star in your own AI videos
stories.simple_summary: Google is adding a feature to its video-creation tool that lets you make videos starring a digital version of yourself. You can describe what video you want, and AI will generate it.
stories.why_it_matters: If you run a small business or want to create marketing videos without filming yourself, this could save you time and money—but it's still new and mostly for people comfortable experimenting with new tools.

stories.title: Roblox launches an AI-powered game-creation feature in its mobile app
stories.simple_summary: Roblox (a platform where people create games) now lets you type a description and have AI automatically make a basic game for you.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect most small business owners or older adults—it's mainly for young people interested in game creation.

stories.title: New York governor says she’s using AI to analyze ‘every single rule’ in the state
stories.simple_summary: New York's governor announced she's using AI to read through all the state's rules and regulations to find ones that are outdated or no longer make sense.
stories.why_it_matters: This could eventually lead to simpler rules for businesses and less paperwork, but it's too early to know what will actually change.

stories.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
stories.simple_summary: Google's AI chatbot can now do tasks in other apps you use—like scheduling, sending messages, or other actions—instead of just answering questions.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for tech enthusiasts right now; ordinary users likely won't notice changes yet.

stories.title: Google is renaming NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
stories.simple_summary: Google is renaming its note-taking app from NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, but it works the same way—it's just getting a new name to fit with Google's other AI products.
stories.why_it_matters: This doesn't affect what the tool does or how you'd use it; it's just a name change.

stories.title: Yes, you can now order DoorDash from the command line
stories.simple_summary: DoorDash created a special tool that lets computer programmers (and their AI assistants) order food through text commands instead of using the website or app.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only for computer programmers and tech companies—nothing that affects ordinary people or small business owners.

stories.title: Why is OpenAI selling a ChatGPT basketball?
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released a basketball with ChatGPT branding on it, though the article doesn't explain why or what it does.
stories.why_it_matters: This appears to be a novelty product or merchandise—it doesn't affect your daily life or business.

stories.title: How a former DeepMind researcher raised at a $300M pre-seed valuation before launching a product
stories.simple_summary: A former AI researcher raised $300 million from investors to start a company focused on visual AI (making AI better at understanding images and video).
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors—it's a significant business development but nothing you need to act on right now.

stories.title: Why AMI Labs’ Alexandre LeBrun won’t call his AI ‘AGI’ or ‘superintelligence’
stories.simple_summary: A startup CEO working on AI thinks people use overhyped words like 'superintelligence' when describing AI, and he prefers not to use them.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly interesting to AI enthusiasts and investors; the debate about terminology doesn't affect ordinary people.

stories.title: Moonshot’s upcoming Kimi 3 is expected to close the gap with Anthropic’s Opus 4.8
stories.simple_summary: A Chinese AI company is building a large language model (computer program that understands and writes language) expected to be as good as other leading AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies competing in AI—it doesn't directly affect your day-to-day life.

stories.title: Apple Intelligence approved for launch in China with Alibaba and Baidu
stories.simple_summary: Apple made a deal with Chinese companies Alibaba and Baidu to provide AI services for Apple products in China.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Apple products in China, this means Apple Intelligence features will work there; if you're elsewhere, this doesn't affect you.

stories.title: Claude can now use your 1Password credentials for you
stories.simple_summary: Claude (an AI chatbot) can now access your passwords from 1Password (a password manager) so it can log into accounts and complete tasks like booking travel without you typing in your information.
stories.why_it_matters: This is convenient if you use both services and trust AI with your passwords, but many people will feel uncomfortable letting AI access their login credentials.
